items.title: Phones in Hand, India’s Young Protesters Blast Through Modi’s Media Control
items.author: Mujib Mashal
items.content: <article>Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s tightly constructed image is being punctured as a Gen Z-led protest movement uses videos and memes to seize the digital narrative.</article>

items.title: The Cost of Trump’s New Tariffs, and the $100 Million Popcorn Bucket Market
items.author: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Alex Travelli and Anupreeta Das
items.content: <article>Plus, the Friday news quiz.</article>

items.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
items.author: Laura Stevens
items.content: <article>Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.</article>

items.title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient U.S. Economy
items.author: Tony Romm
items.content: <article>As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.</article>

items.title: How a Grieving Trump Decided to Back Lindsey Graham’s Sister for Senate
items.author: Emily Davies
items.content: <article>The president initially backed a little-known congressman. Then he talked to Gov. Henry McMaster and Ms. Graham herself.</article>

items.title: Democrats Prepare for Possible Election Clashes With Federal Government
items.author: Nick Corasaniti
items.content: <article>Top officials overseeing state elections and law enforcement are stepping up plans to prepare for what they worry could be interference in the midterms.</article>

items.title: Is the Amaury Family, Owners of the Tour de France, Hurting Cycling?
items.author: David Segal
items.content: <article>The Amaurys of Paris own the race, but won’t share any of the $170 million in media revenue. Sponsors and riders say their resistance is dooming the sport.</article>

items.title: ‘We’re Trapped in Hell’: Iranians Say Life Is Put On Hold by Months of War
items.author: Leily Nikounazar and Erika Solomon
items.content: <article>Devastated by cycles of violence and economic crisis, many are giving up on work and their futures as the United States batters Iran again.</article>

items.title: Platner’s Top Campaign Strategist on What Went Wrong
items.author: Ezra Klein, Rollin Hu, Annie Galvin and Marie Cascione
items.content: <article>Morris Katz helped Zohran Mamdani win, and then tried to do the same for Graham Platner. What has he learned?</article>

items.title: Venezuela’s Earthquakes Show Wealth Divide, Even in Death
items.author: Emma Bubola and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
items.content: <article>Latin America’s deep-seated inequalities prevailed over the grimly equalizing force of the Venezuela earthquakes, and set even the dead apart.</article>

items.title: In ‘The Odyssey’ and ‘Oppenheimer,’ Nolan Knows Civilization Is at Stake
items.author: Alissa Wilkinson
items.content: <article>In very different films, the director’s penchant for playing with time is in service to a larger concern about human bonds across eras.</article>

items.title: Voters Think All Politicians Are a Little Corrupt, but Trump Might Be Pushing It
items.author: Charles Homans
items.content: <article>There’s a difference between ordinary influence peddling and the first family’s outright self-enrichment.</article>

items.title: Can This New Enzyme Turn Back the Clock in Bodily Tissues?
items.author: K. R. Callaway
items.content: <article>In a recent study, scientists devised a way of reversing the buildup of compounds that lead to some age-related diseases.</article>

items.title: Our Vast Collective Jeffrey Epstein Failure
items.author: Rachel Louise Snyder
items.content: <article>We need to hold accountable the web of accomplices who enabled his crimes.</article>

items.title: In Kyrgyzstan, Girls Compete at Kok-Boru, a Game Like Polo
items.author: Alexander M. Thompson and Sevindj Nurkiyazova
items.content: <article>Here’s what happened when some teenage girls dared to compete in the dangerous sport of “kok-boru.”</article>

items.title: Faced With Heat and Wildfires, France Turns to Spain to Learn How to Cope
items.author: Mark Landler
items.content: <article>A French government delegation will travel to its southern neighbor to learn how it copes with long spells of hot weather.</article>

items.title: Jimmy Fallon Pokes Fun at Trump’s ‘Pinky Herman’
items.author: Trish Bendix
items.content: <article>“You got to give Trump credit: He misspeaks in ways nobody ever thought possible,” Fallon said of the president’s calling the famous man-child character “Pinky,” instead of “Pee-wee,” Herman.</article>

items.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
items.author: Ana Swanson
items.content: <article>The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.</article>

items.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
items.author: Tony Romm, Edison Wu and Lazaro Gamio
items.content: <article>The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.</article>

items.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
items.author: Helene Cooper, Lara Jakes and John Ismay
items.content: <article>Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.</article>

items.title: A Fallen Soldier’s Angst, Love and Longing to Return Home From the Iran War
items.author: Jacey Fortin
items.content: <article>Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton was killed in recent days, one of 18 U.S. soldiers who have lost their lives since the beginning of the war in Iran.</article>

items.title: Prominent Indian Activist Breaks Hunger Strike After 26 Days
items.author: Pranav Baskar
items.content: <article>The activist, Sonam Wangchuk, said that he had broken his fast “after long negotiations” with officials and “in view of possible violence in the country.”</article>

items.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
items.author: Michael M. Grynbaum and Jonah E. Bromwich
items.content: <article>A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.</article>
